strategy_justification: Probe confirmed uc_chrome_none works. Anti-bot (Akamai) detected — standard Playwright is blocked. UC Chrome (undetected-chromedriver) bypasses Akamai's fingerprint detection.
proxy_justification: Direct connection with UC Chrome works (uc_chrome_none). No proxy needed on first attempt.

warmup_instructions.step_2: Wait 15-20 seconds for Akamai sensor data collection and challenge resolution
warmup_instructions.step_3: Accept cookie consent — adidas shows a full-page cookie wall that blocks page rendering until accepted. Use data-auto-id='accept-cookie-btn' button or fallback to buttons with text 'Accept All' / 'Accept all cookies'

title.note: JSON-LD ProductGroup block contains name. Also available via data-auto-id product-title attribute per Adidas DOM conventions.

description.note: Description is in JSON-LD. May contain HTML that needs cleaning.

brand.note: Static value 'adidas' from JSON-LD brand field.

price.note: JSON-LD offers are EMPTY. Price must come from CSS or __NEXT_DATA__. Adidas PDP renders price in the DOM. The selector [data-auto-id='product-price'] or .gl-price__value should work. As a primary method, extract from __NEXT_DATA__ JSON embedded in page (script#__NEXT_DATA__). The price is in productStore.products[key].data.priceInfo.currentPrice or similar path. Falls back to CSS selectors if __NEXT_DATA__ unavailable.

original_price.note: Only present when product is on sale. Not available in JSON-LD (empty offers). Extract from __NEXT_DATA__ priceInfo.previousPrice or CSS fallback.

currency.note: adidas.ie is the Ireland storefront — currency is EUR (€).

availability.note: Availability label text or presence of 'Add to Bag' button. If add-to-bag button exists, product is in stock. Can also be extracted from __NEXT_DATA__ productStore.

sku.note: ProductGroupID in JSON-LD serves as SKU identifier.

images.note: Product images are in carousel with data-auto-id attributes. Also check JSON-LD hasVariant[].image if available. Extract src attribute from img elements.

rating.note: aggregateRating present in JSON-LD with ratingValue and reviewCount.

color.note: Color is typically shown as a label on the color swatch selector. May also be in page title (e.g., 'Black').

size.note: Selected size can come from URL parameter forceSelSize or from the selected size variant element in DOM.

next_data_extraction.note: Adidas uses Next.js. Script#__NEXT_DATA__ contains a JSON object with full product data including price, availability, sizes, colors, and images in props.pageProps.initialState.productStore. This is the PRIMARY source for price and availability since JSON-LD offers are empty.
next_data_extraction.important: __NEXT_DATA__ is only available AFTER cookie consent is accepted. The cookie wall blocks full page rendering including __NEXT_DATA__. Accept cookies FIRST, then extract __NEXT_DATA__.

cookie_consent_handling.note: Adidas shows a full-page cookie consent overlay that must be dismissed before any product data is accessible. Use JavaScript click on the accept button. The page will not render product content until consent is given.

js_execution_notes.no_arrow_functions: CRITICAL: When using SeleniumBase/undetected-chromedriver execute_script, do NOT use arrow function IIFEs like (() => { return x; })(). Use var-based top-level statements with return: 'return document.title;'
js_execution_notes.wait_for_rendering: After accepting cookies, wait 2-3 seconds for React/Next.js to hydrate and render product content.
js_execution_notes.price_extraction_js: Use var-based JS to parse __NEXT_DATA__ and extract product data.

confidence_notes: Strategy verified via probe (uc_chrome_none works). JSON-LD fields verified. Price/availability selectors not directly verified (Playwright blocked) but follow Adidas' known DOM conventions with data-auto-id attributes. __NEXT_DATA__ is the recommended primary extraction method for price. Confidence is medium-high — strategy is solid, selectors need runtime verification in the scraper.
